notes: Subscription variant (developer's Pro/Max session). The key user-observable signal is that RateLimitSnapshot is non-nil after the first turn completes — proven by a rate_limit metric event in events.jsonl carrying at least one Window entry. Claude Code does not expose PlanType (the statusline hook carries no plan_type field); the subscription signal is solely the presence of the rate_limits block. The phase arc is a standard single-turn arc; the rate_limit assertion is evidenced by events.jsonl payload content, not by a separate phase. The statusline hook fires on its own tick interval inside the Claude Code CLI; the recording must keep the session alive after the turn until at least one statusline POST arrives at the daemon.

text: Session detected and appears in ready state
User prompt starts a turn; state transitions ready → working

Turn completes; state returns to ready. After this phase, events.jsonl must contain a rate_limit metric event with Windows[] non-empty — the subscription signal. SampledAt is set; UsedPercent ≥ 0 for at least one window.
